steps.title: Place for one with the first event
steps.content: The founding of the League in 1929 is one pivotal event, and the Katzenbach v. Morgan decision is another. The phrase for one should attach to the founding date, then the next event should begin a new sentence.

steps.title: Avoid comma splices
steps.content: Choice A makes for one introduce the court decision instead. Choices B and C create comma splices, and Choice C also omits the founding year. The remaining choice punctuates the first example and starts a new sentence for the second. The answer is D.
